credit_significance: The core senior secured obligation. Rate 8.00% + 3-month SOFR; interest-only, quarterly; matures 2029-05-08. Minimum-liquidity covenant (>= $15M per loan agmt; 10-Q says minimum cash/equivalents). In full compliance at 2025-12-31 & 2026-03-31. Prepayment premium 10%/5%/0% + first-year make-whole.
notes: Principal amount was redacted [***] in the loan agreement; 10-K/10-Q disclose $38.66M gross. Proceeds refinanced the prior 2027 (BioPharma Credit) term loans. No amortization; interest-only until 2029.

relationship: Obligor = parent; secured (junior to term loan)
asset_or_obligation: Revenue participation: 5.0% (6.5% Udenyca step-up) of first tier / 0.5% above tier of U.S. net sales; cap 2.25x purchase price per product
credit_significance: LOQTORZI portion remains: net carrying $14.4M at 2026-03-31 ($14.0M at 2025-12-31); effective rate ~27.4%. A cash-flow leakage claim on LOQTORZI net sales, but junior to the term loan.
notes: Original purchase price $37.5M (Udenyca+Loqtorzi). UDENYCA portion bought out for $47.7M on 2025-04-15 (post-UDENYCA sale). CoC/product-disposition triggers put at Purchase Price less payments (Acceptable Assignee) or 2.25x less payments (otherwise), payable within 5 business days.

relationship: Issuer = parent; unsecured; no subsidiary guarantees

notes: Royalty passes through even the Apotex Canada sublicense. Reduces LOQTORZI cash available to service the term loan.

entity_name: Where LOQTORZI IP and cash flows reside
credit_significance: LOQTORZI U.S./Canada license and U.S. BLA sit at the parent (a Credit Party, within collateral). The underlying molecule IP is owned by Junshi (external). LOQTORZI net sales accrue at the parent, then leak via Junshi royalty (low-20s%) and the Coduet RPR (5.0%/0.5%).
source_file: Derived from Loan Agreement, RPR PSA, Indenture, 10-K, 10-Q
notes: Cash flow reaches the borrower, but two senior-ish economic claims (Junshi royalty off the top; RPR participation) sit ahead of/alongside residual margin. The pipeline IP (casdozokitug, tagmokitug) sits in guarantor Surface Oncology, LLC - inside collateral.

credit_significance: No. The entire Credit Party group is Delaware/domestic; there are no foreign subsidiaries. The 65% voting-stock pledge cap (IRC Sec. 956) and Foreign Subsidiary Holdco concepts are present in the loan docs but inapplicable today.
source_file: Derived from Loan Agreement, RPR PSA, Indenture, 10-K, 10-Q
notes: Positive for lenders: no value trapped in unpledged/unguaranteed foreign entities; no CFC leakage. Confirmed from Loan Agmt Sec. 5.12(d) and the all-Delaware entity roster (EX-21.1 + loan preamble).

credit_significance: Low structural subordination: all material subsidiaries are guarantors and grantors, so the term lenders are not structurally junior to operating-company creditors. Leakage is contractual, not structural: Junshi royalty, LOQTORZI RPR, and Surface CVRs. Convertible notes were structurally subordinated but are essentially retired.
source_file: Derived from Loan Agreement, RPR PSA, Indenture, 10-K, 10-Q
notes: Main leakage channels are the Junshi royalty (unavoidable, senior economics) and the RPR (junior to the term loan). No 'trapped value' in non-guarantor entities. The RPR and CVRs are capped/contingent.

entity_name: Do lenders control equity of key subsidiaries?
credit_significance: Yes. The parent pledges 100% of the equity of every non-Excluded subsidiary, including Surface Oncology, LLC (which holds the pipeline IP). No 65% foreign cap applies (no foreign subs).
source_file: Derived from Loan Agreement, RPR PSA, Indenture, 10-K, 10-Q
notes: Lenders can foreclose on subsidiary equity as well as subsidiary assets - a belt-and-suspenders package. Equity pledge terms are in the redacted Guaranty & Security Agreement but the pledge obligation is explicit in Sec. 5.12(a).

entity_name: Weakest point in the lender collateral & guarantee package
credit_significance: WEAKEST LINK: single-product concentration on an IN-LICENSED asset. Post-divestitures, the credit's value rests overwhelmingly on LOQTORZI, whose molecule IP is owned by Junshi and whose supply depends on Junshi. If the Junshi license/supply fails, the collateral (a license + BLA) is sharply impaired and the pipeline (early-stage Surface assets) cannot cover the term loan.
source_file: Derived from Loan Agreement, RPR PSA, Indenture, 10-K, 10-Q
notes: Secondary weaknesses: (a) key economics (molecule IP, manufacturing) sit outside the collateral with Junshi; (b) full Excluded Property list and equity-pledge mechanics are in the redacted Security Agreement (diligence gap); (c) thin liquidity runway against a large accumulated deficit and continuing net losses. Mitigants: all-domestic all-guarantor structure, 100% equity pledge, substantially-all-assets lien senior to the RPR, minimal senior debt (~$38.7M) and nearly-retired converts, and $167.0M liquidity at 2026-03-31.
